sections_cited: Section 19 of the Civil Servants Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 744 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Disposed of matter/Directions 1. For orders on CMA No.5890/2023 (Ex./A) 2. For orders on CMA No.5891/2023 (Contempt App) 08.11.2023 Petitioner is present in person. Mr. Khuda Bux Chohan, Advocate for respondents-Pakistan Railways. Mr. Dareshani Ali Haider ‘Ada’, Deputy Attorney General..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- This petition, filed for seeking service benefits including commutation, gratuity etc., was disposed of by this Court vide order dated 02.05.2023 in the terms whereby respondents viz. Pakistan Railways were directed to decide application of the petitioner filed for sanctioning of compassionate allowance, as provided under Sub-Section of Section 19 of the Civil Servants Act, 1973, within ninety days. Thereafter, petitioner filed listed application (CMA No.5891/2023) alleging contempt of the Court’s order on the part of respondents. However, learned Counsel for the respondents has submitted a copy of the order dated 04.08.2023 passed by Divisional Superintendent, Sukkur dismissing the representation of the petitioner. Hence, we are of the view that no contempt of the Court’s order has been committed, and if the petitioner is aggrieved by the aforesaid order, he may approach the relevant forum for redressal of his grievance. Accordingly, listed application is disposed of. A copy of the order dated 04.08.2023 is supplied to the petitioner today, and therefore, limitation period for an appeal, if any, would start running from today. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
